text.title: Spec-Driven Debugging Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Spec-Driven Debugging application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Write a failing test that captures the bug BEFORE diagnosing. Test is the spec; passing test = bug fixed + regression-protected.
rule.rationale: Tests-after-fix tend to overfit the fix; tests-first capture the bug's behavior.

rule.statement: Minimize: strip inputs, mocks, libraries until only the failing path remains. Bug fixed in minimal repro = fixed everywhere.
rule.rationale: Large repros hide root causes; minimization is the discovery accelerator.

rule.statement: Commit the failing test first, fix in subsequent commit. Git log = bug story.
rule.rationale: Two-commit pattern documents the bug + makes blame analysis trivial.

rule.statement: Fix narrowest path that satisfies the test. Avoid drive-by refactors in fix commits; track them separately.
rule.rationale: Drive-by changes mask the actual fix; narrow fix + separate refactor PRs.

rule.statement: Bug-test joins the regression suite permanently. Removal requires explicit decision + linked-from-PR rationale.
rule.rationale: Bug tests are the highest-value tests (caught real bug once); removing them silently is risk.
